text.title: RAG Document Ingestion Rules
text.summary: Rules for chunking strategy by document type, required metadata fields, deduplication, and index freshness for RAG pipelines.

rule.statement: Use chunk_size=512 for Q&A docs, 1024 for technical manuals, and 1500 for research papers. Use 20% overlap between chunks.
rule.rationale: Chunk size must balance retrieval precision (smaller = more targeted) with context completeness (larger = fewer split concepts). Document type determines the natural unit of meaning.

rule.statement: Test chunk size empirically on a representative sample of queries before committing to production parameters.
rule.rationale: Optimal chunk size is corpus-dependent. The defaults above are starting points; retrieval quality should be measured on the actual query distribution.

rule.statement: Add metadata at index time: source filename, page number, section heading, and ingestion date. This cannot be added retroactively without re-indexing.
rule.rationale: Metadata enables filtering, citation generation, and staleness detection. Adding it after indexing requires full re-embedding of all documents, which is expensive and causes downtime.

rule.statement: Remove near-duplicate chunks before indexing (cosine similarity > 0.97). Duplicate chunks waste storage and bias retrieval toward repeated content.
rule.rationale: Duplicate chunks appear multiple times in top-k results, crowding out diverse relevant content and inflating context with redundant text.

rule.statement: Implement incremental indexing: track document hashes, re-index only changed documents.
rule.rationale: Full re-indexing on every update is expensive at scale. Document hash comparison identifies changed documents in O(n) and limits embedding API cost to the changed subset.

example.code:
from llama_index.core import SimpleDirectoryReader, VectorStoreIndex from llama_index.core.node_parser import SentenceSplitter
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader( input_dir="./data", recursive=True, required_exts=[".pdf", ".md", ".txt"], exclude_hidden=True, ).load_data()
parser = SentenceSplitter( chunk_size=1024, # technical manuals chunk_overlap=200, # 20% overlap paragraph_separator="\n\n", )
nodes = parser.get_nodes_from_documents(documents)
example.note: chunk_overlap is 200 (20% of 1024). Adjust chunk_size per document type.
